skill.name: working-with-spreadsheets
skill.description: Creates and edits Excel spreadsheets with formulas, formatting, and financial modeling standards. Use when working with .xlsx files, financial models, data analysis, or formula-heavy spreadsheets.
skill.summary: Build and edit Excel spreadsheets with formulas, formatting standards, and verification guidance.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and shell execution patterns, but review found these were false positives from Markdown examples and spreadsheet text. The only retained risk factor is low-severity external command guidance because the skill asks users to run local Python verification commands.

low_findings.title: Benign Local Command Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill includes instructions to run local Python commands for spreadsheet recalculation and verification. The included verifier reads SKILL.md and checks expected sections, with no network, secret, or destructive behavior found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command guidance is real, but the reviewed script only performs local file checks and exits with status codes. There is no evidence of command injection, network access, or secret handling.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Dismissed as Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick findings map to frontmatter text, spreadsheet examples, inline code, Markdown code fences, and Excel error labels. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, reconnaissance commands, or Ruby shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review confirms the flagged patterns are documentation syntax or spreadsheet terminology. The analyzed files contain no matching dangerous runtime behavior.

content.value_statement: Spreadsheet automation often breaks formulas, formatting, and review workflows. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create Excel files with formulas, formatting, and verification steps.
actual_capabilities: Creates Excel workbooks with openpyxl examples.
Shows how to write formulas instead of hardcoded calculated values.
Documents financial model color standards for inputs, formulas, links, and assumptions.
Explains number formats for currency, percentages, valuation multiples, and years.
Guides editing existing worksheets, adding sheets, rows, columns, and cross-sheet formulas.
Provides a checklist for formula verification and common Excel error handling.
limitations: It does not include the referenced recalc.py helper script.
openpyxl writes formulas but does not calculate formula results.
It focuses on .xlsx workflows, not Google Sheets or CSV-only pipelines.
It provides examples and guidance, not a complete spreadsheet application.

use_cases.title: Build a Financial Model
use_cases.description: Create a workbook with input cells, formulas, number formats, and standard financial model colors.

use_cases.title: Prepare Spreadsheet Reports
use_cases.description: Turn analysis data into a formatted Excel report with formulas and readable worksheet layout.

use_cases.title: Maintain Existing Workbooks
use_cases.description: Edit worksheets, add analysis tabs, and preserve formulas while updating model structure.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Workbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an .xlsx workbook with one sheet named Model. Add revenue, growth, and total rows. Use formulas where calculations are needed.

prompt_templates.title: Format a Financial Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a financial model worksheet using blue font for inputs, black font for formulas, green font for linked cells, and clear number formats.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an Existing Workbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the existing workbook by adding an Analysis sheet, linking key cells from the Data sheet, and preserving formulas during the edit.

prompt_templates.title: Verify Formula Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this workbook plan for formula risks. Identify possible #REF!, #DIV/0!, #VALUE!, and #NAME? issues before creating the file.

output_examples.input: Create a sales forecast workbook with revenue, growth, and gross margin.
output_examples.output: A workbook plan with separate input and formula cells, percentage and currency formats, and clear color coding for review.

output_examples.input: Update an existing workbook with a new analysis tab.
output_examples.output: A step-by-step edit plan that loads the workbook, creates the tab, links source cells, formats outputs, and saves a modified file.

output_examples.input: Check a model for formula errors before delivery.
output_examples.output: A verification checklist covering sample references, row and column mapping, denominator checks, and common Excel error types.

best_practices: Use Excel formulas for calculations so reviewers can trace logic.
Apply consistent color and number formatting before sharing a workbook.
Run formula verification after creating or modifying a workbook.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode calculated values when a formula should remain visible.
Do not save a workbook loaded with data_only=True if formulas must be preserved.
Do not assume DataFrame row numbers match Excel row numbers.

faq.question: What spreadsheet formats does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Excel .xlsx files created or edited with Python libraries such as openpyxl and pandas.

faq.question: Can it calculate formula results?
faq.answer: No. It explains that openpyxl writes formulas but does not calculate them. External recalculation is still needed.

faq.question: Does it support financial model formatting?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents color standards for inputs, formulas, cross-sheet links, external links, and key assumptions.

faq.question: Can it edit existing Excel files?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how to load a workbook, modify cells, insert rows, delete columns, add sheets, and save changes.

faq.question: Does it include a verification script?
faq.answer: It includes a simple script that validates the skill documentation. The referenced spreadsheet recalculation helper is not included.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found only low-risk local command guidance and no evidence of malicious behavior.
